chunks.content:
Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
[]: How is Shinji responsible for *anything*? (Free Will in NTE)             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Oct 20, 2013 9:29 am [Split from "How is Shinji responsible for 3rd Impact?" - Monk] ElMariachi wrote: THat would be completely insane to even consider give him another chance at this state.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 20, 2013 9:57 am Kendrix wrote: I'd hardly call "locking someone away to rot without any intentions to let them out of their quanrantine cell or give them anything to do and threatening to blow them up at the slightest provocation" anything close to "giving someoe a chance."

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Oct 20, 2013 11:17 am ElMariachi wrote: But anyway my point is that WILLE didn't deemed useful/human to execute him, if he "only" provoked one apocalypse that understandable, if his N3I wasn't the direct responsible of said apocalypse that's even more understandable, but if it already happened twice before? The mere fact that they get him out of a cell without being restrained head to toe would be strange... but now that he'd done it a third time ? THat would be completely insane to even consider give him another chance at this state.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 20, 2013 1:00 pm monitoradiation wrote: What happened in 3.33 was that Misato let him actually choose.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Oct 20, 2013 3:22 pm ElMariachi wrote: Wait, what choice are you talking about? He was in custody and she threatened to kill him is he chose to escape with Mark.09, sure afterward she couldn't bring herself to execute the "punishment" for disobeying her command, but that doesn't change the fact that Shinji didn't had any real choice in 3.33 either, at least until the moment he was the only one controlling EVA-13 and had the choice to pull out the spears or not(and Gendo broke his mind thoroughly to maximize the chances that he will pull out the spears)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
 controlling EVA-13 and had the choice to pull out the spears or not(and Gendo broke his mind thoroughly to maximize the chances that he will pull out the spears)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Oct 20, 2013 3:48 pm She was pointing the trigger at him. If that's not a threat, nothing is. The visual similarity to a gun is hardly gonna be unintentional on Anno's part; That's the psychological effect it's supposed to have. Misato didn't let him go because she thought it was a wise thing to do or conciously chose to do so; It's because she underestimated her remaining attachment to the kid. She very much wanted to kill him, she was planning to, but she couldn't emotionally do it. Look at her, she's completely in this "firing pose", her whole body turned, arms streched out, legs posed etc, but the goddamn finger just trembles and refuses to push down. You're probably reading too much into the organization name, there's nothing to suggest that it's intended to be seen in the context of " free will " as a concept(That adjective is nowhere in the organization name), bur rather "The Will to resist/live" that was mentioned so often in EoE, or just random continuation of the theme naming.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Oct 20, 2013 5:16 pm Kendrix wrote: She was pointing the trigger at him. If that's not a threat, nothing is. The visual similarity to a gun is hardly gonna be unintentional on Anno's part; That's the psychological effect it's supposed to have. Misato didn't let him go because she thought it was a wise thing to do or conciously chose to do so; It's because she underestimated her remaining attachment to the kid. She very much wanted to kill him, she was planning to, but she couldn't emotionally do it.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 20, 2013 5:23 pm @ monitoradiation : as Kendrix said, Misato was telling him to don't go anywhere while pointing the activated trigger at his face(even shaped like a gun to strengthen the feeling that she threatening him of death), and in any case Shinji still didn't had any choice : had he decided to listen to Misato and stayed, Mark

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
 while pointing the activated trigger at his face(even shaped like a gun to strengthen the feeling that she threatening him of death), and in any case Shinji still didn't had any choice : had he decided to listen to Misato and stayed, Mark.09 would had surely taken him by force. As for the choice of piloting the Eva, sure he decided by his own volition to pilot again to try to repair the world... after Kaworu presented to him a plan to fix everything and then when he still refused to go, heavily guilt-tripped him by removing the DSS Choker and putting it on himself. And here again Shinji didn't actually had a choice : had he refused, Kaworu would had continued to try to convince him to pilot with him, and if that failed too, Gendo and SEELE would had got impatient and used more forceful means, like brainwashing(Fuyu and Gendo were already discussing the possibility as soon as in 1.0 if Shinji refused to participate in operation Yashima) And in 1.11 and 2.22, he also didn't had a choice, but not in the way you think : Misato is the chief of operations of NERV, so if she said that Shinji can resign, that means that they have means to manage with that : find another pilot, transfer Asuka to Japan to pilot EVA-01 while EVA-02 is shipped to Tokyo-3, after all, in Rebuild cores can be reconfigured to be adapted to the pilot, sure it's not the same thing for Unit 01 since Yui is the control system, but Misato didn't knew that. But the more important is that Shinji couldn't get away from this situation because Gendo wouldn't had let him : remember that unlike in NGE were he called Shinji because he didn't had any other choice while Rei was wounded, here Shinji is a crucial part of Gendo's plans to awake EVA-01 with Rei, and as I said earlier, Gendo was ready to brainwash him to go to fight Ramiel again, so he would had probably do the same if Shinji resigned at any other moment. That was until the Dummy System was complete, there apparently Gendo could use it to awake EVA-01(unless he did planned to have Shinji kidnapped and brainwashed after he left the city!), but since at that moment Yui decided to tell to his husband to go fuck

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
, there apparently Gendo could use it to awake EVA-01(unless he did planned to have Shinji kidnapped and brainwashed after he left the city!), but since at that moment Yui decided to tell to his husband to go fuck himself with his IAs, in the end Shinji was the only one left to pilot to fight Zeruel. During all the the first three movies, Shinji only had the illusion of choice, which boiled down to do what Gendo wanted him to do for his plan willingly or to be forced to do it. Shinji has been Gendo's puppet for his whole life, and that's why now in FINAL, for the first time in his life, Shinji is truly free from Gendo's shadow since he lost any use in his father's eyes. That's also why I really hope that Misato will show some understanding and leniency toward Shinji's situation and will give him the choice of what he want to do from now on to help mankind(be it to fight alongside WILLE or be brought back to a civilian life to continue his life and be a farmer/factory worker/whatever) this time without any pressure, guilt-trip or other manipulation(something even Kaworu was guilty of), so whatever Shinji chose(and in he end he will probably chose to fight, even if it's as an afterthought) will be his own choice, aware of what he must do and what's at stake. Kendrix wrote: You're probably reading too much into the organization name, there's nothing to suggest that it's intended to be seen in the context of " free will " as a concept(That adjective is nowhere in the organization name), bur rather "The Will to resist/live" that was mentioned so often in EoE, or just random continuation of the theme naming.

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Oct 20, 2013 5:58 pm ElMariachi wrote: @ monitoradiation : as Kendrix said, Misato was telling him to don't go anywhere while pointing the activated trigger at his face(even shaped like a gun to strengthen the feeling that she threatening him of death), and in any case Shinji still didn't had any choice : had he decided to listen to Misato and stayed, Mark.09 would had surely taken him by force.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 20, 2013 6:34 pm

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
 choice : had he decided to listen to Misato and stayed, Mark.09 would had surely taken him by force.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Oct 20, 2013 6:34 pm @ monitoradiation : what I'm trying to say is that unrelated to Shinji's choices and apparent free will, in the end his choices didn't had any consequences but the one planned by obscures forces unknown to him(i.e. Gendo), be it by manipulating him to maximize the chances of him doing what Gendo wants. Tthat's why he paraded an half-dead Rei to make him pilot, and brought them close so Shinji would do everything,even die, to save her, triggering EVA-01's awakening, and 14 years later by sending Rei Q with Mark.09 to retrieve Shinji, betting that he will think that it's poka Rei with EVA-00 rescuing him from this den of madman that is the Wunder, and finally crushing him under the guilt of what happened to the world and the fact he didn't saved Rei to make him obsessed to find a way to atone to the point of complete delusion, which will make him pull out the spears. And if he refuses to do any of that? Brainwashing. To retake your metaphor, a more accurate one would be that your friends suggests you to watch 3.33 on Sunday by telling you that watching animes the Sunday is better because you are more rested than during the week... but if you decide that you'll wait Monday to see it at the anime club, then they will tie you to a chair and force you to see it the Sunday, even if you didn't know that you'll be forced to see it the Sunday when you made the choice to wait the Monday, in the end you still saw it the Sunday. That's exactly how Shinji's situation really was though the three first movies, the only moment where he really had a choice was when in Lilith's Chamber when Asuka ended out of battery and he cut off Kaworu's commands... and that's probably why Gendo was so overkill in breaking his son's mind to maximize the chances of him choosing to still make the gamble of pull out the spears even when Kaworu noticed that it was a trap and was begging him to stop. So in the end Shinji's freedom of choice was only an illusion, since whatever he choses, the choice will

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
 still make the gamble of pull out the spears even when Kaworu noticed that it was a trap and was begging him to stop. So in the end Shinji's freedom of choice was only an illusion, since whatever he choses, the choice will be imposed to him if it doesn't suit Gendo's plan. In the end, Shinji was a slave to Gendo, indeed ignorant of his condition, but a slave nonetheless. And that's why I'm saying that Misato giving a choice to Shinji without trying to manipulate him into choosing a certain path and forcing him to take the one which is more convenient for her would be a very powerful symbol for FINAL, and definitively and unambiguously set WILLE as the good guys. Besides Misato was already doing that as soon as in 1.0 : when she brought him to Lilith's Chamber so Shinji would see that even the other people at NERV are risking their lives in this war, it was just so Shinji had all the informations before making his choice(something no one else did), and she was still saying that she will accept whatever decision he will make. That's why she thanked him from the bottom of her heart later when he accepted to participate in operation Yashima. Sure Shinji actually didn't had a choice, since Gendo would had forced him pilot anyway via brainwashing, but neither Misato nor Shinji knew that, meaning that Misato's acceptance of whatever decision Shinji would have taken were genuine, and now in FINAL she is in a position to give him a real choice without someone behind the curtain ready to impose his will to him if he doesn't chose "correctly". And no, I don't think that she will magnum his face when he comes back in FINAL : she clearly was worried for him when 4I was happening and the Wunder failed to stop EVA-13(meaning that Shinji will die due to the DSS Choker), and besides in this post-apocalyptic world there is absolutely nothing else where Shinji could hide, so if Misato really decides to kill Shinji, he wouldn't have any chance of surviving, and I don't see Anno killing off meaninglessly the main protagonist like that! After all by the master's own admission Rebuild is Shinji's story!(even if he admitted that he wanted to tell a "Tale" for Rebuild, the main POV through which we're seeing the events unfolding is Shinji's)

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
 the master's own admission Rebuild is Shinji's story!(even if he admitted that he wanted to tell a "Tale" for Rebuild, the main POV through which we're seeing the events unfolding is Shinji's)

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Sun Oct 20, 2013 8:49 pm ElMariachi wrote: @ monitoradiation : what I'm trying to say is that unrelated to Shinji's choices and apparent free will, in the end his choices didn't had any consequences but the one planned by obscures forces unknown to him(i.e. Gendo)

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Oct 21, 2013 4:06 am I see, now it boils down to see if Anno considers compatibilism as something... well, compatible with the Evangelion setting. Sure Shinj's decisions to pilot to spare Rei the burden to do it when she was half dead, to stay and fight Ramiel and the subsequent Angel, as well as trying to save Rei from Zeruel were still important and meaningful since they were genuine, but what I'm saying is that his "destiny" wasn't in his hand, because there always was his father's shadow stirring him toward a certain decision(which thankfully could be considered as heroic, until the moment it ended in EVA-01 awakening and wrecking Tokyo-3 and later to fell in a trap with the two spears) and even was ready to force it out of him if Shinji decides something else. And I think that Anno doesn't think that compatibilism is a good thing, since WILLE, the good guys, are fighting Gendo and SEELE to let mankind decide of their own destiny instead of having it imposed to them. After all having humanity decide that staying in the form of Lilin is better than evolving into another lifeform through the HIP is nice and all, but if in the end SEELE still bring HIP and the forced evolution to their face and evolve them whether mankind want it or not, in the end the Lilin choice loose it's significance. And that trend was also present in EoE : Instrumentality happens, is forced through mankind via Shinji, but in the end he realize that Instrumentality is not what he want and proceed to break it to be back in the world of AT Fields and take his destiny on his own hands.(sure that leave the plot hole that maybe some people found

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
ji, but in the end he realize that Instrumentality is not what he want and proceed to break it to be back in the world of AT Fields and take his destiny on his own hands.(sure that leave the plot hole that maybe some people found Instrumentality to be very enjoyable and wanted to stay, but I'm no sure Anno thought about it, maybe the Rebuild was a way to fix that hole) A compatibilist situation would be that Shinji get his epiphany, realize that actually his time as a being with an AT Field wasn't so bad because his feelings then were real and accept that fact... but can't do anything about it since he won't get out of Instrumentality. Evangelion is partly a coming of age story, and one big part of growing-up is to make you own decisions and build your own path, Shinji can make his own decision(up until the end of 3.0 at least, but I'm sure he will recover enough will in FINAL), now he needs to be able to forge his own path, something impossible before with Gendo carving his path for him(even though Shinji didn't knew that), and that was even the case in 3.0 : Mark.09 would had made him come with her, willingly or not, and Gendo would had made him pilot with Kaworu, willingly or by using coercion. And Misato was very honest with Shinji even in 3.33, even with the "Rei doesn't exist anymore" comment, it's just that Mark.09 appeared before she could explain everything to him. I can't see her being cryptic just for the sake of it, it's possible that she wanted to manage a little Shinji instead of directly dropping the bomb that his actions 14 years basically changed Rei's prison from Zeruel's core to EVA-01's, and there was a lot of other things to discuss with him : like the fact that he's not in NERV but in WILLE, that they fight Gendo who is an enemy of mankind and actually always was, that Rei was one of many clones of his mother(if they know that bit about Rei's "donor"), that the world is now a giant toxic ruin and that apparently he had an hand in it, for what exactly he's being punished(which ties with the previous point)... that makes a lot of things to explain in a very short time, and info-dumping him on one go would just make him believe that she

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
 he had an hand in it, for what exactly he's being punished(which ties with the previous point)... that makes a lot of things to explain in a very short time, and info-dumping him on one go would just make him believe that she lost her mind and/or is trying to trick him with some insane story... which is exactly what he believed when she desperately tried to convince him to stay with them when Mark.09 appeared. Finally, it's very ambiguous if Misato was the one who detonated the DSS Choker at the end of the movie, remember that it started to glow before the Wunder rammed EVA-13. The most logical think is that it activated automatically once EVA-13 awakened(one if its functions is called "Awakening Analysis Mode"). It's possible that Misato hoped that by dragging EVA-13 far enough of the Door of Guf with the Wunder's AT Field cage and damaging it enough with main canons, the Eva will deactivate and 4I stops, which will allow for Shinji's life to be spared. But the plan was thwarted by Mark.09 damaging and hacking the ship, letting EVA-13 escape and resumes its position at the center of the Door, hence Misato knowing that now the only thing that can stop EVA-13 and 4I is the DSS Choker killing the pilot(apparently she didn't know that Shinji wasn't the one wearing ti anymore) And being sad or worried isn't the point here, the point is that this scene, alongside the one in which she couldn't detonate the Choker, serves to show that under that cold exterior, she still has feelings toward Shinji and want to prevent as much as possible to have to kill him. That would be weird to have her wanting to kill him on the spot in FINAL after that! Besides seeing Shinji's situation, WILLE and the Lilin is the only place he can go now, so if Misato wants him dead, nothing will be able to save Shinji from that, and having the main characters' head be blown off at the beginning of the final movie will makes the rest of his story kinda moot, no? Besides the preview explicitly said that Shinji will find hope again in the place he ends up, hard to find hope when the person you considers as a mother wants your head, no? monitoradiation wrote: Thinking about this - it might be that there's a reason why they have to keep a BM alive

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
 in the place he ends up, hard to find hope when the person you considers as a mother wants your head, no? monitoradiation wrote: Thinking about this - it might be that there's a reason why they have to keep a BM alive - It could be that Shinji's soul will automatically return to the template within Eva-01 and his presence will make it impossible for Wille to use Eva-01 as an energy source. That's why they don't kill him. They need him out of Eva-01.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Oct 21, 2013 4:49 am I just want to mention, that despite this "Shinji finding hope" promise, it still means nothing. Shinji found hope in the form of Kaworu's plan as well. And we get to "enjoy" seeing this hope blast the world and turn Shinji into zombie. Shinji finding hope in FINAL means nothing, since Anno can just use it to break him again. And again. And again. Until he accepts the only one and single proper paragon of morals Anno wants to see. A form of brainwashing and mental conditioning. Beaten so hard that he accepts whatever reality and values are forced on him as his own, unable to recognize his own will anymore. Just instead by human hands it will be done by divine karma, so no one actually forces him, right?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Mon Oct 21, 2013 5:25 am ^ Since EoE was about Shinji finding his own path and going toward it by deciding that reality was better than Instrumentality and getting out of it, I would find it strange if suddenly Rebuild's message is the polar opposite, that he mustn't seek his own path, but bend to whoever other's will and moral. As for Kaworu, he said it himself that there is always hope, always , even if the one in which he and Shinji bet is revealed to have been sabotaged by Gendo, that only means that Shinji must search somewhere else to find that hope, see Kaworu's last words, that now Shinji needs peace and a place to call his own(i.e. somewhere to call "home"), that his road isn't over yet. Anno's "paragon of moral" is that someone must find his own path, but that it doesn't mean to close himself to people who have chosen

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
 own(i.e. somewhere to call "home"), that his road isn't over yet. Anno's "paragon of moral" is that someone must find his own path, but that it doesn't mean to close himself to people who have chosen another one or comes from another group. He used the otakus for this message in his interviews : he doesn't want them to abandon their ways to become proper members of the society, since himself doesn't recognize himself in said society, but that it doesn't mean that they should close themselves into their own circle. From ehre it would be really odd if the remake of his masterpiece is revealed to have an highly conservative message where the protagonist must be subservient to the government/parental figure/authority/whatever, don't yu think?

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Oct 21, 2013 5:52 am It would not be strange. I said before, Anno is playing god, and treats Evangelion as a Bible. Fills it with cryptic messages and symbols, tortures the characters for the sake of some "message" and gives often contradictive signals. So it would NOT be surprising nor unusual for me. It does not help Shinji grow up or evolve. It forces him to a certain path with karmic kicks, by stacking absurd amount of situational odds that points in only one "proper" direction. But we don't see humans directly forcing it, so it gives an illusion of choice. In reality, it forces Shinji into certain box without posiibility of flexibility. Everything he do will have bad outcome unless it follows exact karmic path, to a single designed "proper" outcome. I call this way of "character developement" hollow.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Oct 21, 2013 6:47 am I actually see a huge shift there, both in Misato's attitude towards Shinji, and the general circumstances; The first two movies are full with displays of her respect and trust in him, and her attempts to raise him to be a person who pursues their goals properly instead of just going with the flow. In Q, he isn't even considering what he thinks for an instant, or how the way she's treating him affects him - basically, the world is hash and there's not enough time and ressources to mind the intrests of every little individual, and that's her new idology,

Article: How is Shinji responsible for anything Free Will in NTE
 thinks for an instant, or how the way she's treating him affects him - basically, the world is hash and there's not enough time and ressources to mind the intrests of every little individual, and that's her new idology, not just in regards to Shinji. Even when she spared him, it was because of her circumstances, who she couldn't control or chose. Sure, averting impacts has priority, but that was just the same when she had to make him pilot - but she tried her best to contain and mitigate the damage. Basically, just when Shinji decides to get his act together, the universe decides to turn on him and withdraw all support, and all the encouraging people offering him shiny rewards for getting into the robot. Before, there were basically....not guarantees of success, but at least chances of how he might have done it better, to underline this point of making use of chances instead of doing nothing because you're afraid of doing it wrong. They were there precisely because Shinji wouldn't be using them for the fullest - if he were able to at the time, this would be a very different show. Anno's reasoning is probably closer to, "Congratulations, Shinji, you have reached level two, which is obviously harder than the first one. Now, prove that you don't need the pampering anymore." The odds in this show where always overwehelmingly bad and painfully unfair. That's it being EVA, the show where "favorable odds" is having anything other than zeroes left of the point.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Mon Oct 21, 2013 7:25 am So yeah. Basically Shinji was born handicapped, and Q as the next level, took away his crutches to make him crawl. While everyone treat him like a tool or a disease. That suppose to make him a better person how? That's why if Shinji realises in FINAL how horrible shit he is and that he should spend rest of his life repenting for being born, and do it with a smile on his face because he will be in a "I deserve it" state, I will barf.
